clause_quote: This Powertrain Warranty applies only to vehicles with an active powertrain warranty mapping recorded against the VIN at the time of claim review.
The powertrain policy covers warrantable defects in listed powertrain components when the repair order date and mileage fall within the applicable limits.
If no active VIN-to-warranty mapping exists for the claimed vehicle, the claim is not eligible for powertrain coverage.
The Powertrain Warranty provides coverage for 60 months from the in-service date or 100,000 km, whichever occurs first.
A claim is within the time limit when the repair order date is on or before the warranty end date recorded for the VIN.
A claim is within the mileage limit when the odometer reading at repair order opening is at or below the recorded mileage ceiling.
Covered powertrain components include the engine assembly, cooling system, water pump, thermostat, radiator, transmission internal parts, and drive axle assembly.
A claimed repair code must map to the stated causal part and to a covered powertrain component for the claim to remain eligible.
A repair code and causal part mismatch is a data integrity failure and the claim shall not be auto-approved.
Powertrain coverage applies only when the failure results from a defect in material or workmanship.
Leakage, internal breakage, bearing failure, or seal failure may be warrantable when the description identifies the failed part and the defect condition.
A generic statement that a part was replaced, without the defect found and the correction performed, is incomplete and requires more information.
Powertrain coverage does not apply to failures caused by abuse, racing, unauthorized modification, collision damage, normal wear, or contaminated fluids.
If the failure description directly attributes damage to an excluded cause, the claim shall be rejected under this policy.
When exclusion evidence is suggestive but not conclusive, the claim shall be referred for human review rather than automatically approved.
Every powertrain claim must include the VIN, in-service date, repair order date, odometer reading, repair code, causal part, and a specific failure description.
Powertrain claims beyond 36 months from the in-service date require complete maintenance records before approval.
Claims with missing mandatory documentation shall not be approved and must be returned for completion or referred for review.
Engine assembly and transmission internal assembly replacements require prior approval before the claim may be approved.
A major assembly replacement claim must include a teardown summary or equivalent technical explanation identifying the failed internal condition.
If prior approval or the required technical explanation is missing for a major repair, the claim requires more information.
Labor reimbursement is evaluated against the published standard and maximum labor time for the repair code.
A claim within the published labor limit may proceed to standard adjudication when all other conditions are satisfied.
Labor time above the published maximum requires human review and cannot be auto-approved.
A powertrain claim shall be approved only when coverage, component mapping, failure eligibility, documentation, and labor limits are all satisfied.
A powertrain claim shall be rejected when objective eligibility fails because the vehicle is outside the warranty period, above the mileage limit, or excluded by cause.
A powertrain claim shall be referred to human review when the evidence is conflicting, ambiguous, or suggests a possible exception outside strict rules.
Goodwill may be considered only when the claim fails warranty solely on time or mileage limits by a small margin and no exclusion applies.
Goodwill consideration requires complete maintenance history and a defect pattern consistent with a warrantable failure.
Goodwill does not convert a rejected warranty claim into an approved warranty claim and must be referred for human authorization.
